title: U.S. GAO - Critical Minerals: Reducing U.S. Import Reliance with Substitution and Recycling Technologies
excerpt: The U.S. relies on imports for many critical minerals that are essential to the battery and semiconductor industries. But their supply chains are...
content_html:
Skip to main content

Fast Facts

The U.S. relies on imports for many critical minerals that are essential to the battery and semiconductor industries. But their supply chains are vulnerable to disruptions.

We looked at how substitution and recycling technologies might reduce reliance on imports. Battery recycling—extracting minerals from batteries for reuse—could reduce imports in 2 to 3 years. Policy options that could help reach this goal include establishing infrastructure for collecting and recycling materials.

By contrast, substitution and recycling may not reduce short-term import reliance in the semiconductor industry.

An electronic chip with small rock-like items on it.

Skip to Highlights

Highlights

What GAO Found

Technologies like batteries and semiconductors are essential to the U.S. economy but rely on imports of lithium, gallium, and other critical minerals. GAO found that substitution and recycling technologies could help reduce U.S. reliance on these imports. But progress is likely to take many years in some cases.

Potential for technologies to reduce critical mineral import reliance

For batteries, substitution and recycling technologies offer near-term potential. For example, using lithium iron phosphate batteries for stationary grid energy storage might reduce imports of cobalt, manganese, and nickel in the next 2 to 3 years. But these batteries do not perform as well in other applications, like long-haul electric vehicles, due to their lower energy density. Lithium-free batteries are not yet commercially mature. Battery recycling technologies are mature and offer a pathway to reduce imports for copper, cobalt, lithium, and nickel in 2 to 3 years. These technologies aim to recover critical minerals at high rates through chemical leaching and smelting. Experts told GAO, however, that U.S. battery recyclers lack capacity. Additionally, available inputs for recycling (referred to as feedstock) are often landfilled or exported for processing.

For semiconductors, most substitution and recycling technologies are likely years away from maturity. Substitution is unlikely to have a near-term effect on import reliance of minerals, such as gallium and indium, because other materials do not perform as well as these critical minerals across the same conditions. Experts told GAO that industry will not adopt substitutes until they can perform at the same level as current semiconductor materials in the intended application. Technologies to recycle minerals from semiconductors also face challenges, such as an underdeveloped market. Semiconductor manufacturing scrap is a potential source of recycled minerals, though this is not yet standard practice in the U.S. Discarded electronics are another potentially large source, but their critical mineral content is low, and those minerals are generally mixed and bonded with other materials. Technologies to recycle this form of electronic waste are in pilot-stage development.

GAO identified four policy options that could support the goal of reducing critical mineral import reliance and help address challenges to recycling and substitution technologies. These options identify possible actions by policymakers, which include legislative bodies, government agencies (federal, state, and local), academia, standards-setting organizations, industry, and other groups. In general, these technologies have potential, with policy action, to reduce some of this reliance in the near, medium, or long term. See below for details on these options.

Policymakers could also choose to pursue non-technological policy approaches, such as increased domestic mining. Many non-technological approaches are the subject of current and proposed legislation and executive action (see report p. 6).

Policy options to support reducing critical mineral import reliance and address challenges to substitution and recycling technologies

Establish domestic manufacturing capacity for viable substitutes (report p. 23)

Policymakers could consider building or repurposing existing manufacturing capacity to produce substitute technologies for batteries and semiconductors.

Potential implementation approaches

  • Providing support for private capital investments in manufacturing plants for commercially available battery technologies.
  • Establishing facilities or partnerships to perform pilot-scale testing of semiconductor substitutes.

Opportunities and Considerations

  • Domestic manufacturing capacity could allow for near-term production of commercially available substitute technologies like lithium iron phosphate batteries, which could reduce import reliance on critical minerals like cobalt and nickel.
  • Battery and semiconductor production facilities are costly and tend to be specialized for the production of a particular technology.

Establish domestic recycling capacity (report p. 24)

Policymakers could build domestic infrastructure to bolster the domestic capacity to recycle batteries and semiconductors.

Potential implementation approaches

  • Providing support for private investment in factories for new battery recycling.
  • Collecting and sharing data on manufacturing scrap and end-of-life devices containing batteries and semiconductors.
  • Reviewing and streamlining permitting requirements and hazardous waste designations.

Opportunities and Considerations

  • Expanded domestic infrastructure could reduce or eliminate the need to ship partially processed batteries overseas for recycling.
  • Building domestic recycling infrastructure may not reduce import reliance for selected critical minerals in the near term, since building new or repurposing existing infrastructure can take a decade or more and require significant investment.

Secure inputs for recycling (report p. 24)

Policymakers could support efforts to collect, sort, transport, and store manufacturing scrap and end-of-life devices for recycling and reuse.

Potential implementation approaches

  • Establishing and overseeing national and local electronic waste collection programs that provide education and resources (e.g., drop-off locations) for consumers.
  • Providing financial incentives for industry to collect or retain manufacturing scrap and end-of-life devices for recycling.

Opportunities and Considerations

  • Securing a consistent supply of inputs for recycling and reuse could help reduce the need for critical mineral importation in the near term.
  • Although mature battery recycling technologies exist, securing additional inputs may exacerbate challenges related to the safety, transportation, and handling of hazardous materials.
  • Without demand and competitively priced recycled semiconductor materials, it may be difficult for recyclers to scale their operations and offset the amount of imported minerals required in the near term.

Support research, development, and testing (report p. 25)

Policymakers could continue or grow support for research, development, and testing of substitution and recycling technologies.

Potential implementation approaches

  • Supporting research to improve performance of lithium-free batteries.
  • Supporting research into battery recycling technologies that reduce or avoid the cost, waste issues, and environmental impact of current techniques.
  • Encouraging targeted materials research focused on semiconductor substitutes that maintain or enhance material performance and reduce the use of critical minerals when possible.
  • Supporting research on more efficient semiconductor recycling technologies that recover more critical minerals.

Opportunities and Considerations

  • Increased research could help overcome current limitations of existing technologies, resulting in better performance (e.g., higher energy density batteries, larger bandgap semiconductors) or higher commercialization potential (e.g., improved recovery of minerals from semiconductors).
  • Research, development, and testing efforts may not yield mature technologies in the near term.

Source: GAO. | GAO-26-108687

Why GAO Did This Study

The U.S. considers 60 minerals as critical because they are essential to the nation’s economy or security and have supply chains vulnerable to disruption.

Several critical minerals are key to the functioning of batteries and semiconductors, which have applications in electric vehicles, stationary grid energy storage, consumer electronics, and the defense industry. For example, lithium is a key component of modern batteries that are both lightweight and energy-dense. Semiconductors rely on the unique electrical properties of critical minerals such as gallium, germanium, arsenic, and indium.

U.S. executive and legislative branch policymakers have a long-standing goal to reduce critical mineral import reliance, through technological innovations and other approaches.

This report examines: (1) substitution and recycling technologies to reduce import reliance on critical minerals within the battery and semiconductor industries, (2) challenges to the development and adoption of these technologies, and (3) policy options to support reducing reliance.

To conduct this technology assessment, GAO interviewed federal officials and experts and reviewed academic papers, agency and expert documentation, and federal policy. GAO identified four policy options in this report (see next page).

For more information, contact Sarah Harvey at HarveyS@gao.gov.

Full Report

Topics

RecyclingSemiconductorsStrategic mineralsImportsManufacturingMineralsResearch and developmentEnergy storageGeological surveysData centers

sentences.en: For batteries, substitution and recycling technologies offer near-term potential.
sentences.zh: 对于电池而言，替代和回收技术提供了近期潜力。

sentences.en: For example, using lithium iron phosphate batteries for stationary grid energy storage might reduce imports of cobalt, manganese, and nickel in the next 2 to 3 years.
sentences.zh: 例如，使用磷酸铁锂电池用于固定电网储能可能会在未来 2 至 3 年内减少钴、锰和镍的进口。

sentences.en: But these batteries do not perform as well in other applications, like long-haul electric vehicles, due to their lower energy density.
sentences.zh: 但由于能量密度较低，这些电池在长途电动汽车等其他应用中表现不佳。

sentences.en: Lithium-free batteries are not yet commercially mature.
sentences.zh: 无锂电池在商业上尚未成熟。

sentences.en: Battery recycling technologies are mature and offer a pathway to reduce imports for copper, cobalt, lithium, and nickel in 2 to 3 years.
sentences.zh: 电池回收技术已经成熟，为2至3年内减少铜、钴、锂和镍的进口提供了途径。

sentences.en: These technologies aim to recover critical minerals at high rates through chemical leaching and smelting.
sentences.zh: 这些技术旨在通过化学浸出和熔炼高速回收关键矿物。

sentences.en: Experts told GAO, however, that U.S. battery recyclers lack capacity.
sentences.zh: 然而，专家告诉政府问责局，美国电池回收商缺乏能力。

sentences.en: Additionally, available inputs for recycling (referred to as feedstock) are often landfilled or exported for processing.
sentences.zh: 此外，可用于回收的投入（称为原料）通常会被填埋或出口进行加工。

blocks.html_en: For semiconductors, most substitution and recycling technologies are likely years away from maturity. Substitution is unlikely to have a near-term effect on import reliance of minerals, such as gallium and indium, because other materials do not perform as well as these critical minerals across the same conditions. Experts told GAO that industry will not adopt substitutes until they can perform at the same level as current semiconductor materials in the intended application. Semiconductor manufacturing scrap is a potential source of recycled minerals, though this is not yet standard practice in the U.S. Discarded electronics are another potentially large source, but their critical mineral content is low, and those minerals are generally mixed and bonded with other materials. Technologies to recycle this form of electronic waste are in pilot-stage development.

sentences.en: For semiconductors, most substitution and recycling technologies are likely years away from maturity.
sentences.zh: 对于半导体来说，大多数替代和回收技术可能还需要数年时间才能成熟。

sentences.en: Substitution is unlikely to have a near-term effect on import reliance of minerals, such as gallium and indium, because other materials do not perform as well as these critical minerals across the same conditions.
sentences.zh: 替代不太可能对镓和铟等矿物的进口依赖产生短期影响，因为在相同条件下其他材料的性能不如这些关键矿物。

sentences.en: Experts told GAO that industry will not adopt substitutes until they can perform at the same level as current semiconductor materials in the intended application.
sentences.zh: 专家告诉 GAO，除非替代品能够在预期应用中达到与当前半导体材料相同的水平，否则业界不会采用替代品。

sentences.en: Technologies to recycle minerals from semiconductors also face challenges, such as an underdeveloped market.
sentences.zh: 从半导体中回收矿物的技术也面临着挑战，例如市场不发达。

sentences.en: Semiconductor manufacturing scrap is a potential source of recycled minerals, though this is not yet standard practice in the U.S. Discarded electronics are another potentially large source, but their critical mineral content is low, and those minerals are generally mixed and bonded with other materials.
sentences.zh: 半导体制造废料是回收矿物的潜在来源，尽管这在美国尚未成为标准做法。废弃电子产品是另一个潜在的大来源，但其关键矿物含量较低，而且这些矿物通常与其他材料混合和粘合。

sentences.en: Technologies to recycle this form of electronic waste are in pilot-stage development.
sentences.zh: 回收这种形式的电子废物的技术正处于试点开发阶段。

sentences.zh: 美国政府问责局确定了四项政策选择，可以支持减少关键矿产进口依赖的目标，并帮助应对回收和替代技术的挑战。

sentences.en: In general, these technologies have potential, with policy action, to reduce some of this reliance in the near, medium, or long term.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，通过政策行动，这些技术有潜力在近期、中期或长期减少这种依赖。

sentences.en: See below for details on these options.
sentences.zh: 有关这些选项的详细信息，请参阅下文。

  • Domestic manufacturing capacity could allow for near-term production of commercially available substitute technologies like lithium iron phosphate batteries, which could reduce import reliance on critical minerals like cobalt and nickel.
  • Battery and semiconductor production facilities are costly and tend to be specialized for the production of a particular technology.

blocks.html_en:
Establish domestic recycling capacity (report p. 24)

Policymakers could build domestic infrastructure to bolster the domestic capacity to recycle batteries and semiconductors.

sentences.en: Establish domestic recycling capacity (report p.
sentences.zh: 建立国内回收能力（报告第 17 页）

sentences.en: 24) Policymakers could build domestic infrastructure to bolster the domestic capacity to recycle batteries and semiconductors.

blocks.html_en:
Potential implementation approaches

  • Providing support for private investment in factories for new battery recycling.
  • Collecting and sharing data on manufacturing scrap and end-of-life devices containing batteries and semiconductors.
  • Reviewing and streamlining permitting requirements and hazardous waste designations.

sentences.zh: 审查和简化许可要求和危险废物名称。

blocks.html_en:
Opportunities and Considerations

  • Expanded domestic infrastructure could reduce or eliminate the need to ship partially processed batteries overseas for recycling.
  • Building domestic recycling infrastructure may not reduce import reliance for selected critical minerals in the near term, since building new or repurposing existing infrastructure can take a decade or more and require significant investment.

sentences.en: Opportunities and Considerations Expanded domestic infrastructure could reduce or eliminate the need to ship partially processed batteries overseas for recycling.
sentences.zh: 机遇和考虑因素扩大国内基础设施可以减少或消除将部分加工的电池运送到海外进行回收的需要。

Secure inputs for recycling (report p. 24)

Policymakers could support efforts to collect, sort, transport, and store manufacturing scrap and end-of-life devices for recycling and reuse.

Potential implementation approaches

  • Establishing and overseeing national and local electronic waste collection programs that provide education and resources (e.g., drop-off locations) for consumers.
  • Providing financial incentives for industry to collect or retain manufacturing scrap and end-of-life devices for recycling.

blocks.html_en:
Opportunities and Considerations

  • Securing a consistent supply of inputs for recycling and reuse could help reduce the need for critical mineral importation in the near term.
  • Although mature battery recycling technologies exist, securing additional inputs may exacerbate challenges related to the safety, transportation, and handling of hazardous materials.
  • Without demand and competitively priced recycled semiconductor materials, it may be difficult for recyclers to scale their operations and offset the amount of imported minerals required in the near term.

sentences.en: Opportunities and Considerations Securing a consistent supply of inputs for recycling and reuse could help reduce the need for critical mineral importation in the near term.
sentences.zh: 机遇和考虑因素 确保回收和再利用投入的持续供应可能有助于减少短期内对关键矿物进口的需求。

sentences.en: Although mature battery recycling technologies exist, securing additional inputs may exacerbate challenges related to the safety, transportation, and handling of hazardous materials.
sentences.zh: 尽管存在成熟的电池回收技术，但确保额外的投入可能会加剧与危险材料的安全、运输和处理相关的挑战。

sentences.en: Without demand and competitively priced recycled semiconductor materials, it may be difficult for recyclers to scale their operations and offset the amount of imported minerals required in the near term.
sentences.zh: 如果没有需求和价格具有竞争力的回收半导体材料，回收商可能很难扩大业务规模并抵消短期内所需的进口矿物数量。

blocks.html_en:
Support research, development, and testing (report p. 25)

Policymakers could continue or grow support for research, development, and testing of substitution and recycling technologies.

blocks.html_en:
Potential implementation approaches

  • Supporting research to improve performance of lithium-free batteries.
  • Supporting research into battery recycling technologies that reduce or avoid the cost, waste issues, and environmental impact of current techniques.
  • Encouraging targeted materials research focused on semiconductor substitutes that maintain or enhance material performance and reduce the use of critical minerals when possible.
  • Supporting research on more efficient semiconductor recycling technologies that recover more critical minerals.

sentences.en: Potential implementation approaches Supporting research to improve performance of lithium-free batteries.
sentences.zh: 潜在的实施方法 支持提高无锂电池性能的研究。

sentences.en: Supporting research on more efficient semiconductor recycling technologies that recover more critical minerals.
sentences.zh: 支持研究更有效的半导体回收技术，以回收更多关键矿物。

blocks.html_en:
Opportunities and Considerations

  • Increased research could help overcome current limitations of existing technologies, resulting in better performance (e.g., higher energy density batteries, larger bandgap semiconductors) or higher commercialization potential (e.g., improved recovery of minerals from semiconductors).
  • Research, development, and testing efforts may not yield mature technologies in the near term.

sentences.en: Opportunities and Considerations Increased research could help overcome current limitations of existing technologies, resulting in better performance (e.g., higher energy density batteries, larger bandgap semiconductors) or higher commercialization potential (e.g., improved recovery of minerals from semiconductors).
sentences.zh: 机遇和考虑因素增加研究有助于克服现有技术的当前局限性，从而获得更好的性能（例如，更高能量密度的电池、更大带隙的半导体）或更高的商业化潜力（例如，提高半导体中矿物的回收率）。

sentences.en: Research, development, and testing efforts may not yield mature technologies in the near term.
sentences.zh: 研究、开发和测试工作可能不会在短期内产生成熟的技术。

blocks.html_en: Why GAO Did This Study

sentences.en: Why GAO Did This Study
sentences.zh: GAO 为何进行这项研究

blocks.html_en: The U.S. considers 60 minerals as critical because they are essential to the nation’s economy or security and have supply chains vulnerable to disruption.

sentences.en: The U.S. considers 60 minerals as critical because they are essential to the nation’s economy or security and have supply chains vulnerable to disruption.
sentences.zh: 美国认为 60 种矿产至关重要，因为它们对国家经济或安全至关重要，而且供应链容易受到干扰。

blocks.html_en: Several critical minerals are key to the functioning of batteries and semiconductors, which have applications in electric vehicles, stationary grid energy storage, consumer electronics, and the defense industry. For example, lithium is a key component of modern batteries that are both lightweight and energy-dense. Semiconductors rely on the unique electrical properties of critical minerals such as gallium, germanium, arsenic, and indium.

sentences.en: Several critical minerals are key to the functioning of batteries and semiconductors, which have applications in electric vehicles, stationary grid energy storage, consumer electronics, and the defense industry.
sentences.zh: 几种关键矿物对于电池和半导体的功能至关重要，这些矿物在电动汽车、固定电网储能、消费电子产品和国防工业中都有应用。

sentences.en: For example, lithium is a key component of modern batteries that are both lightweight and energy-dense.
sentences.zh: 例如，锂是现代电池的关键组成部分，既轻质又高能量。

sentences.en: Semiconductors rely on the unique electrical properties of critical minerals such as gallium, germanium, arsenic, and indium.
sentences.zh: 半导体依赖于镓、锗、砷和铟等关键矿物的独特电特性。

blocks.html_en: U.S. executive and legislative branch policymakers have a long-standing goal to reduce critical mineral import reliance, through technological innovations and other approaches.

sentences.en: U.S. executive and legislative branch policymakers have a long-standing goal to reduce critical mineral import reliance, through technological innovations and other approaches.
sentences.zh: 美国行政和立法部门政策制定者的长期目标是通过技术创新和其他方法减少对关键矿产进口的依赖。

blocks.html_en: This report examines: (1) substitution and recycling technologies to reduce import reliance on critical minerals within the battery and semiconductor industries, (2) challenges to the development and adoption of these technologies, and (3) policy options to support reducing reliance.

sentences.en: This report examines: (1) substitution and recycling technologies to reduce import reliance on critical minerals within the battery and semiconductor industries, (2) challenges to the development and adoption of these technologies, and (3) policy options to support reducing reliance.
sentences.zh: 本报告研究了：(1) 替代和回收技术，以减少电池和半导体行业对关键矿物的进口依赖，(2) 开发和采用这些技术面临的挑战，以及 (3) 支持减少依赖的政策选择。

blocks.html_en: To conduct this technology assessment, GAO interviewed federal officials and experts and reviewed academic papers, agency and expert documentation, and federal policy. GAO identified four policy options in this report (see next page).

sentences.en: To conduct this technology assessment, GAO interviewed federal officials and experts and reviewed academic papers, agency and expert documentation, and federal policy.
sentences.zh: 为了进行这项技术评估，政府问责局采访了联邦官员和专家，并审查了学术论文、机构和专家文件以及联邦政策。

sentences.en: GAO identified four policy options in this report (see next page).
sentences.zh: 美国政府问责局在本报告中确定了四个政策选项（见下页）。

sentences.zh: 欲了解更多信息，请联系 Sarah Harvey：HarveyS@gao.gov。

content_html_zh:
Skip to main content

速览

美国依赖进口许多对电池和半导体行业至关重要的关键矿物。 但他们的供应链很容易受到干扰。

我们研究了替代和回收技术如何减少对进口的依赖。 电池回收——从电池中提取矿物质进行再利用——可以在 2 到 3 年内减少进口。 有助于实现这一目标的政策选择包括建立收集和回收材料的基础设施。

相比之下，替代和回收可能不会减少半导体行业的短期进口依赖。

上面有类似岩石的小物体的电子芯片。

跳至亮点

亮点

GAO 发现了什么

电池和半导体等技术对美国经济至关重要，但依赖锂、镓和其他关键矿物的进口。 美国政府问责局发现替代和回收技术可以帮助减少美国对这些进口产品的依赖。 但在某些情况下，进展可能需要很多年。

减少关键矿产进口依赖的技术潜力

对于电池而言，替代和回收技术提供了近期潜力。 例如，使用磷酸铁锂电池用于固定电网储能可能会在未来 2 至 3 年内减少钴、锰和镍的进口。 但由于能量密度较低，这些电池在长途电动汽车等其他应用中表现不佳。 无锂电池在商业上尚未成熟。 电池回收技术已经成熟，为2至3年内减少铜、钴、锂和镍的进口提供了途径。 这些技术旨在通过化学浸出和熔炼高速回收关键矿物。 然而，专家告诉政府问责局，美国电池回收商缺乏能力。 此外，可用于回收的投入（称为原料）通常会被填埋或出口进行加工。

对于半导体来说，大多数替代和回收技术可能还需要数年时间才能成熟。 替代不太可能对镓和铟等矿物的进口依赖产生短期影响，因为在相同条件下其他材料的性能不如这些关键矿物。 专家告诉 GAO，除非替代品能够在预期应用中达到与当前半导体材料相同的水平，否则业界不会采用替代品。 从半导体中回收矿物的技术也面临着挑战，例如市场不发达。 半导体制造废料是回收矿物的潜在来源，尽管这在美国尚未成为标准做法。废弃电子产品是另一个潜在的大来源，但其关键矿物含量较低，而且这些矿物通常与其他材料混合和粘合。 回收这种形式的电子废物的技术正处于试点开发阶段。

美国政府问责局确定了四项政策选择，可以支持减少关键矿产进口依赖的目标，并帮助应对回收和替代技术的挑战。 这些选项确定了政策制定者可能采取的行动，其中包括立法机构、政府机构（联邦、州和地方）、学术界、标准制定组织、行业和其他团体。 总的来说，通过政策行动，这些技术有潜力在近期、中期或长期减少这种依赖。 有关这些选项的详细信息，请参阅下文。

政策制定者还可以选择采取非技术政策方法，例如增加国内采矿业。 许多非技术方法是当前和拟议的立法和行政行动的主题（见报告第 17 页）。 6).

支持减少关键矿产进口依赖并应对替代和回收技术挑战的政策选择

建立可行替代品的国内制造能力（报告第 17 页） 23) 政策制定者可以考虑建设或重新利用现有的制造能力，以生产电池和半导体的替代技术。
潜在的实施方法为商业电池技术制造工厂的私人资本投资提供支持。 建立设施或合作伙伴关系以进行半导体替代品的中试规模测试。 机遇和考虑因素国内制造能力可以在短期内生产磷酸铁锂电池等商用替代技术，这可以减少对钴和镍等关键矿物的进口依赖。 电池和半导体生产设施成本高昂，并且往往专门用于特定技术的生产。
建立国内回收能力（报告第 17 页） 24) 政策制定者可以建设国内基础设施，以增强国内回收电池和半导体的能力。
可能的实施方法 支持私人投资新电池回收工厂。 收集和共享包含电池和半导体的制造废料和报废设备的数据。 审查和简化许可要求和危险废物名称。 机遇和考虑因素扩大国内基础设施可以减少或消除将部分加工的电池运送到海外进行回收的需要。 建设国内回收基础设施可能不会在短期内减少对某些关键矿物的进口依赖，因为建设新的或重新利用现有基础设施可能需要十年或更长时间，并且需要大量投资。
确保回收投入（报告第 17 页） 24) 政策制定者可以支持收集、分类、运输和储存制造废料和报废设备以进行回收和再利用的努力。
可能的实施方法 建立并监督国家和地方电子废物收集计划，为消费者提供教育和资源（例如垃圾收集地点）。 为工业界收集或保留制造废料和报废设备进行回收提供财政激励。 机遇和考虑因素 确保回收和再利用投入的持续供应可能有助于减少短期内对关键矿物进口的需求。 尽管存在成熟的电池回收技术，但确保额外的投入可能会加剧与危险材料的安全、运输和处理相关的挑战。 如果没有需求和价格具有竞争力的回收半导体材料，回收商可能很难扩大业务规模并抵消短期内所需的进口矿物数量。
支持研究、开发和测试（报告第 17 页） 25) 政策制定者可以继续或加大对替代和回收技术的研究、开发和测试的支持。
潜在的实施方法 支持提高无锂电池性能的研究。 支持电池回收技术的研究，以减少或避免现有技术的成本、废物问题和环境影响。 鼓励针对半导体替代品的有针对性的材料研究，以保持或增强材料性能并尽可能减少关键矿物的使用。 支持研究更有效的半导体回收技术，以回收更多关键矿物。 机遇和考虑因素增加研究有助于克服现有技术的当前局限性，从而获得更好的性能（例如，更高能量密度的电池、更大带隙的半导体）或更高的商业化潜力（例如，提高半导体中矿物的回收率）。 研究、开发和测试工作可能不会在短期内产生成熟的技术。

Source: GAO. | GAO-26-108687

GAO 为何进行这项研究

美国认为 60 种矿产至关重要，因为它们对国家经济或安全至关重要，而且供应链容易受到干扰。

几种关键矿物对于电池和半导体的功能至关重要，这些矿物在电动汽车、固定电网储能、消费电子产品和国防工业中都有应用。 例如，锂是现代电池的关键组成部分，既轻质又高能量。 半导体依赖于镓、锗、砷和铟等关键矿物的独特电特性。

美国行政和立法部门政策制定者的长期目标是通过技术创新和其他方法减少对关键矿产进口的依赖。

本报告研究了：(1) 替代和回收技术，以减少电池和半导体行业对关键矿物的进口依赖，(2) 开发和采用这些技术面临的挑战，以及 (3) 支持减少依赖的政策选择。

为了进行这项技术评估，政府问责局采访了联邦官员和专家，并审查了学术论文、机构和专家文件以及联邦政策。 美国政府问责局在本报告中确定了四个政策选项（见下页）。

欲了解更多信息，请联系 Sarah Harvey：HarveyS@gao.gov。
